documentation.description: == ARP Protocol == The Net.Protos.Arp package implements the support for the ARP protocol used for the resolution of IPv4 addresses. The package maintains a ARP database within an Ada protected object. The Resolve procedure is the main entry point for the resolution of the IPv4 address. Given a target IP address, it first looks in the ARP database for the associated Ethernet address. When the Ethernet address is known, it updates the Ethernet header so that the packet can be sent to the network interface. When the Ethernet address is now known, the Resolve procedure puts the packet in a queue and it makes an ARP request. The Receive procedure should be called when a ARP packet is received. It is responsible for replying to ARP requests from other hosts on the network and handling ARP response for our requests. It updates the ARP database only when we receive a ARP response from one of our ARP query.

simple_types.name: Arp_Status
simple_types.code: type Arp_Status is (ARP_FOUND, ARP_PENDING, ARP_NEEDED, ARP_QUEUE_FULL, ARP_UNREACHABLE);

subprograms.name: Receive
subprograms.code:
procedure Receive (Ifnet     : in out Net.Interfaces.Ifnet_Type'Class;
                   Packet    : in out Net.Buffers.Buffer_Type)

subprograms.name: Request
Resolve
Update
